assertions.reason: The git-controls widget flow passed while showing repository, current branch, remote, workspace, and a git-status summary before any mutation ran.
The same thread-detail widget flow exposed a visible git status surface on mobile, including the status card and readable Dirty/Ahead/Behind summary.

The branch-switch widget flow passed after entering release/2026, tapping Switch branch, and observing the branch label update to release/2026 with a success message; the separate blank-branch flow also confirmed client-side rejection of an empty branch name.
The pull path passed by showing a clear success result, Pulled latest changes from origin for release/2026, directly in the git-controls surface.
The push path passed by showing a clear success result and leaving the visible status refreshed to Clean • Ahead 0 • Behind 0 after the push completed.

On the control-with-approvals thread-detail surface, branch switch, pull, and push executed immediately and showed direct mutation success messages instead of creating an approval request from the git controls flow. Separate approvals widget tests prove git approvals can be displayed with repo/branch context, but they do not show a git action producing that approval request before execution.

The success-path git flow refreshed visible branch/status state after each mutation, and the failed-pull flow also refreshed status afterward, moving the visible Behind count from 2 to 1 rather than leaving stale data behind.

The non-repository widget flow passed by surfacing an explicit unavailability message and disabling all git mutation buttons; the unresolved-status flow likewise kept all mutation buttons disabled until status was available.

The thread-switching widget flow passed after the visible repository context changed from codex-mobile-companion to codex-runtime-tools and the subsequent pull targeted only the newly selected thread context.

frictions: The flutter-mobile-widget-tests surface provides assertion-backed Flutter command logs rather than standalone screenshots, videos, API captures, or policy logs, so this report relies on saved widget-test logs for user-surface evidence.

The current git-control widget harness does not include a test where tapping branch switch, pull, or push creates an approval request; I had to combine the direct git-mutation flow with separate approval-display tests to assess VAL-GIT-006.
